preamble: The shift from the voiced palatal approximant /j/ to the alveolar lateral approximant /l/ is a documented historical phonetic change, particularly observed in Oceanic languages. This transformation involves a change in the place of articulation from palatal to alveolar, and a shift in the manner of articulation from a central approximant to a lateral approximant.
phoneticEffects: Place assimilation (palatal to alveolar), manner change (central approximant to lateral approximant), sonority decrease
sources: Bermúdez-Otero, R. (2018). On the diachrony of lateral epenthesis. In The Oxford Handbook of Historical Phonology (pp. 739-756). Oxford University Press.
